reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures "the intersection of any collection of subspaces of V is a subspace of V." The indexed family `u : ι → Submodule F V` is equivalent to "any collection of subspaces" (any set-indexed collection can be viewed as an indexed family and vice versa). The conclusion `∃ U : Submodule F V, (⋂ i, (u i).carrier) = ↑U` precisely expresses that the set-theoretic intersection of the carriers equals the carrier of some submodule — i.e., the intersection is itself a subspace. The typeclass setup `[Field F] [AddCommGroup V] [Module F V]` correctly models "V is a vector space over F". No hypotheses are added or dropped. Mathlib's `CompleteLattice (Submodule R M)` instance confirms this holds unconditionally, including the edge case where ι is empty (intersection = univ = ↑⊤).
